mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of economic uncertainty.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 when applied outside late-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market strength and rising household income, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative forward returns in > 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.3% are followed by positive forward returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly auto sales, where auto sales decline while GDP expands, signals weakening consumer spending momentum relative to aggregate output, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence signals yield positive forward returns in > 50% of instances over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates immediate supply chain disruptions or demand shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Sharp industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.
